title: Raita
subtitle: Cooling yoghurt with cucumber and cumin
excerpt: Plain yoghurt combined with grated cucumber, toasted cumin, and mint, the cooling condiment served alongside hot curries to provide contrast and relief from heat. Raita works both as a dip for starters and as a sauce alongside the main dishes. It is prepared in minutes and requires no cooking beyond toasting the cumin seeds.
sourceNotes: Raita (from the Hindi, from 'rai', meaning mustard) is a yoghurt-based condiment served across South Asia alongside spiced dishes. The cucumber version is the most common variant on British Indian menus. It has been a standard accompaniment since British Indian restaurants opened in the UK.

content.text: Cucumber releases a large amount of water, which will thin the raita within about 30 minutes of mixing. To delay this, salt the grated cucumber lightly and leave it in a sieve for 10 minutes before squeezing out the liquid, then mixing into the yoghurt. This extra step keeps the raita thick and prevents it becoming watery on the table.

The cumin should be toasted dry in a small pan until fragrant, then ground or left whole. Toasting releases the aromatic oils and gives the cumin a nuttier, less raw flavour than untoasted ground cumin. This single step makes a significant difference to the finished raita.

Toast the {{cumin-seeds}} in a dry pan over medium heat for 1 to 2 minutes until fragrant. Crush lightly in a mortar or with the back of a spoon. Squeeze as much liquid as possible from the grated {{cucumber}}. Combine the {{plain-yoghurt}}, cucumber, toasted cumin, {{mint-dried}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}} in a bowl. Stir well. Dust lightly with {{chilli-powder}} if using. Refrigerate until needed.

items.fix: Salt the grated cucumber and drain for 10 minutes before squeezing. Serve within 2 hours of making; drain off any liquid if made further ahead
items.cause: Cucumber moisture not removed or made too far ahead

items.fix: The toasted cumin is the primary flavour. Taste and adjust salt, yoghurt condiments need assertive seasoning
items.cause: Cumin not toasted or insufficient salt

items.fix: Full-fat yoghurt is thicker and holds its body better. Greek yoghurt can be used for an even thicker result
items.symptom: Yoghurt too thin from the start

items.fix: Raita should taste of yoghurt and cucumber first, with cumin as a background note. Reduce to a pinch and taste
items.cause: Too much cumin or not balanced with mint

content.text: Raita is the cooling element in a curry-house meal, its role is contrast rather than flavour complexity. The yoghurt's lactic acid cuts through the fat of the main dishes; the cooling cucumber and mint provide relief alongside hot curries. It is served as a dip with poppadoms and starters, and as a sauce alongside biryani. Without raita, the curry-house meal lacks its regulating element.
